qa_pairs.question: Why would a user choose to set up a ParaView server on a cloud VM instead of an Alliance HPC cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Setting up a ParaView server on a cloud VM is a less common approach, recommended only if a user requires a custom setup that is not supported by the ParaView installations available on the Alliance HPC clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What software packages need to be installed on a CentOS cloud VM before compiling ParaView or VisIt for client-server visualization?
qa_pairs.answer: On a CentOS cloud VM, users need to install packages such as xauth, wget, gcc, gcc-c++, ncurses-devel, python-devel, libxcb-devel, patch, imake, libxml2-python, mesa-libGL, mesa-libGL-devel, mesa-libGLU, mesa-libGLU-devel, bzip2, bzip2-libs, libXt-devel, zlib-devel, flex, and byacc.

qa_pairs.question: Why is OSMesa support essential when compiling ParaView on a cloud VM, and which rendering drivers does it enable?
qa_pairs.answer: OSMesa support is essential because most Arbutus cloud VMs do not have a GPU, requiring ParaView to perform offscreen (software) rendering. Compiling with OSMesa enables OpenSWR for OpenGL and includes both the llvmpipe (older and slower) and SWR (newer and faster) drivers for CPU-based rendering.
